 {"id":"gt-afn0","title":"gt sling: use mail queue for patrol roles (witness, refinery, deacon)","description":"When slinging work to patrol agents, queue via mail instead of replacing hook.

**Rationale:**
Patrol agents run continuous loops. Replacing their hook with discrete work breaks patrol continuity - when the task completes, the patrol stops.

**New behavior for patrol roles (witness, refinery, deacon):**
1. Check if patrol is running (hook has patrol molecule attached)
2. If patrol running:
   - Don't touch hook (patrol stays pinned)
   - Send work assignment mail
   - Print 'Queued for next patrol cycle'
3. If patrol NOT running:
   - Start default patrol for that role (mol-witness-patrol, mol-refinery-patrol, mol-deacon-patrol)
   - Send work assignment mail
   - Print 'Started patrol and queued work'

**New flags:**
- --urgent: Interrupt current patrol cycle, process this work immediately
- --replace: Explicitly terminate patrol and pin discrete work (break-glass)

**No change for:**
- Polecat, Crew, Mayor (discrete task agents - current behavior)

**Dependencies:**
- Patrol templates must have 'check inbox' step (verify/add)
- Need to know default patrol molecule for each role","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-23T16:12:58.431633-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-23T16:24:52.027412-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-23T16:24:52.027412-08:00","close_reason":"Closed"}
 {"id":"gt-ai1z","title":"TODO: Detect cycles in molecule dependency graph","description":"molecule.go:302 has a TODO to detect cycles in the dependency graph. Currently, cyclical dependencies could cause issues.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-21T21:34:28.169096-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T21:48:55.388426-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T21:48:55.388426-08:00","close_reason":"Implemented DFS-based cycle detection in molecule dependency graph with 4 new tests"}
 {"id":"gt-alx","title":"Swarm: ephemeral rig support","description":"PGT has ephemeral rigs for swarms - temporary worker groups that are destroyed after landing.

Missing Features:
- gt swarm init [--rig <name>|--git-url <url>] [--num-workers N]
- gt swarm worker add/remove/list <rig-id>
- gt swarm rigs - List ephemeral rigs
- gt swarm destroy <rig-id> - Destroy ephemeral rig

Directory structure:
<workspace>/mayor/workers/<rig-id>/
├── rig.json (metadata)
├── alice/ (git clone)
├── bob/
└── carol/

PGT Reference: gastown-py/src/gastown/ephemeral.py

Note: Beads issue gt-kmn.12 mentions this but implementation is missing.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-16T14:47:14.302762-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-16T16:03:47.902849-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-16T16:03:47.902849-08:00"}
-{"id":"gt-aobh","title":"Polecats should not bd sync on startup","description":"Polecats all share the same beads database at the rig level. The refinery and mayor/witness manage syncing beads.

## Current Behavior
Polecat startup runs bd sync, causing:
- Contention when multiple polecats spawn simultaneously
- Unnecessary sync operations
- Potential race conditions

## Desired Behavior
- Polecats should NOT run bd sync on startup
- They read from the shared beads database
- Only refinery/witness/mayor sync beads

## Implementation
Remove bd sync from polecat spawn/startup sequence.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-21T16:40:18.705507-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T16:40:18.705507-08:00"}
+{"id":"gt-aobh","title":"Polecats should not bd sync on startup","description":"Polecats all share the same beads database at the rig level. The refinery and mayor/witness manage syncing beads.

## Current Behavior
Polecat startup runs bd sync, causing:
- Contention when multiple polecats spawn simultaneously
- Unnecessary sync operations
- Potential race conditions

## Desired Behavior
- Polecats should NOT run bd sync on startup
- They read from the shared beads database
- Only refinery/witness/mayor sync beads

## Implementation
Remove bd sync from polecat spawn/startup sequence.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"gastown/tracer","created_at":"2025-12-21T16:40:18.705507-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-23T21:44:16.565444-08:00"}
 {"id":"gt-aqd8","title":"Witness Patrol Molecule","description":"Create mol-witness-patrol molecule for witness heartbeat loop.

Similar to DeaconPatrolMolecule but for per-rig witness duties.

## Steps
1. inbox-check - Process witness mail
2. survey-workers - List polecats, check status
3. inspect-workers - Capture pane output, assess state  
4. plugin-run - Execute plugins from <rig>/plugins/witness/
5. decide-actions - Determine nudges, escalations
6. execute-actions - Send nudges, process shutdowns
7. context-check - Check own context, handoff if needed
8. loop-or-exit - Continue or cycle

The plugin-run step enables user customization of the patrol loop.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-21T16:16:52.808202-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-23T04:39:26.805771-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-23T04:39:26.805771-08:00","close_reason":"Superseded by Christmas Ornament pattern (gt-tnow, gt-qflq). Flat patrol design replaced by dynamic bonding with per-polecat arms."}
 {"id":"gt-aqku","title":"BUG: gt done doesn't push branch to origin - work lost when worktree deleted","description":"## Problem

`gt done` creates a merge-request record in beads but **never pushes the branch to origin**.

When polecats are cleaned up (worktrees deleted), the branch is lost forever. The MR record exists but there's no actual branch to merge.

## Evidence

12 MQ items had `ready` status but no corresponding branches on origin. All polecat worktrees were deleted, losing all work.

## Impact

**CRITICAL**: All polecat work is silently lost unless polecats manually push (which they don't know to do).

## Fix

In `done.go`, before creating the MR:
1. `git push origin <branch>` to push the branch to remote
2. Only create MR record if push succeeds

## Location
`mayor/rig/internal/cmd/done.go:124-135`","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-21T17:21:27.012045-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T17:22:45.104108-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T17:22:45.104108-08:00","close_reason":"Fixed in done.go and mq_submit.go - both now push branch before creating MR"}
 {"id":"gt-aqm","title":"Beads as Universal Data Plane","description":"## Vision

Beads is the data plane for all Gas Town operations. Everything flows through beads:
- Work items (issues, tasks, epics)
- Mail (messages between agents)
- Merge requests (queue entries)
- Workflows (composable execution patterns)
- Resources (leases, locks, quotas)
- Schedules (timed activities)

## New Bead Categories

### Molecules (Composable Workflows)
Crystallized workflow patterns that can be attached to work items.

### Timed Beads (Scheduled Work)
Beads that wake up periodically via daemon.

### Pinned Beads (Ongoing Concerns)
Beads representing persistent concerns, not discrete tasks.

### Resource Beads (Leases/Locks)
Beads representing reserved resources.

## v1 Priority

- **P0**: Molecules (enables engineer-in-box)
- **P2**: Timed, Pinned, Resource beads (post-v1)","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-18T18:05:57.847578-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-18T18:06:10.898712-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-aqm","depends_on_id":"gt-4nn","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-18T18:06:29.881371-08:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"gt-aqm","depends_on_id":"gt-caz","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-18T18:08:19.833603-08:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"gt-aqm","depends_on_id":"gt-8h4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-18T18:08:19.930166-08:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"gt-aqm","depends_on_id":"gt-b3p","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-18T18:08:20.026188-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
